methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Medra is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Michelle Lee that has raised $52M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage cohort (including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent at Series B) alongside 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $52M raised proxy, yielding $104M. The peer-median method uses valuations across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies. The median valuation among these peers is $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Medra's revenue is not disclosed, which is typical for private companies. The public sector median P/S of 6.6x therefore remains informational only.

The triangulated range is $88M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $454M. The wide spread reflects the stage heterogeneity within the peer set—most comparable companies are at Series B, further along than Medra's current Series A position. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data and unit economics, which would narrow the valuation corridor and ground the assessment in operational metrics rather than stage-based proxies alone.
